key_insight: A two-stage, continuously fine-tuned machine-learning workflow can capture how amino-silane molecules passivate hybrid halide perovskite surfaces and how surface interactions change with molecular coverage.
engineering_relevance: The approach provides a practical route to design more reliable passivation strategies for perovskite optoelectronic devices using atomistic simulations informed by realistic data.
method: Build a large, curated open dataset of diverse metal and hybrid halide perovskite structures (hyP-26).
Fine-tune the model with a small, specialized dataset for amino-silane adsorption/passivation on the surface.
Use the trained potential to simulate mixed-composition perovskite surfaces across varying molecular coverages.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a multistep training pipeline combining a broad public dataset and a focused passivation dataset, then applying the model to study coverage-dependent surface interactions.
watch_item: Because realistic molecule-on-surface passivation is highly specialized, model transferability outside the curated chemistries and training conditions may be limited.
paper_title: Data-driven atomistic modelling of hybrid halide perovskite passivation
